admission_rule.history_domain: a finite or countably unbounded sequence of effectively indexed events with no frozen terminal length
admission_rule.prefix_access: a total uniform procedure returns every requested finite prefix and its declared annotations
admission_rule.transition_locality: each next event, admissibility judgment, and revision effect is determined from a finite effectively recoverable dependency cone
admission_rule.persistence: prefix embeddings preserve prior event identity, order, grounds, dependencies, and revision records
admission_rule.reasoning_layer: commitments, grounds, dependencies, revisions, evidential states, and rule changes are finitely described at every finite stage
admission_rule.presentation: uniform algorithms for prefix enumeration, dependency-cone recovery, transition checking, and prefix extension
exclusions: steps whose declared justification depends on an unrecoverable infinite past
global revisions that rewrite an unbounded past without an effective finite change certificate
uncountable event domains without effective names

observation_boundary.analyst_access: the frozen effective presentation and any requested finite prefix
observation_boundary.represented_agent_access: only source-declared observations and history available at the represented stage
prohibited: fixed terminal horizon presented as total coverage
undeclared compression that loses event identity or dependency history

nonclaims: the complete future exists as a represented object at every stage
non-effective or uncountable histories are covered
necessity minimality or uniqueness is established
